title: [HWToLLVM] Add support for aggregate constant op
body:
Since this pass is not multi-threaded (within a builtin module) we can maintain a global mapping and construct the globals on-the-fly. If we want to multithread it at some point, we should lower the constant aggregates to llvm globals in a first pass that is not multithreaded and then multithread the rest, or use thread-safe datastructures. I think it should be safe to insert operations outside of the op the pattern matches on as long as we use the provided rewriter (and to not insert the constructed global op unless the matchAndRewrite function is guaranteed to return with a success).

Unfortunately, these changes also required changes in test files (from the LLHD to LLVM lowering) that should not test these kind of things.
While adding tests, I noticed that a lot of unnecessary unrealized conversion cast operations are inserted, so I deleted them as part of this PR to keep the newly added tests simpler.
The `signal-init.mlir` in particular does not test the things that it actually should, but I think that's better left for another PR.
